tasks.title: Apply Supabase Migration and RLS Policies
tasks.description: Run the existing 001_initial_schema.sql migration against the live Supabase instance. Verify all 7 tables, indexes, RLS policies, storage buckets, and triggers are created. Test RLS enforcement with test users.
tasks.details: Use Supabase MCP or CLI to apply supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql. Verify: businesses, team_members, documents, obligations, transactions, bank_statement_uploads, generated_documents tables exist. Test RLS: create two test users, insert data as user A, verify user B cannot read it. Verify storage buckets: documents, receipts, logos, statements.
tasks.testStrategy: Query each table. Insert as user A, attempt read as user B — should return empty. Upload test file to each bucket.

tasks.title: Wire Authentication Flow (Magic Link)
tasks.description: Complete the auth flow: magic link email sending on login/signup pages, callback handling, session persistence, and redirect logic. Users should be able to sign up, receive an email, click the link, and land on the dashboard.
tasks.details: Wire login/signup pages to call supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ email }). Configure Supabase Auth email templates. Verify auth/callback/route.ts exchanges code for session. Add session refresh logic. Add logout button functionality in sidebar. Handle expired sessions gracefully.
tasks.testStrategy: Sign up with a real email. Receive magic link. Click it. Verify redirect to /dashboard. Refresh page — session persists. Click logout — redirected to /login.

tasks.title: Wire Onboarding to Create Real Business Record
tasks.description: Connect the 3-step onboarding wizard to Supabase. Step 1: upload CR image to storage. Step 2: insert business record. Step 3: save contact info. Auto-generate compliance obligations on completion.
tasks.details: Step 1 (CR Upload): Upload file to 'documents' storage bucket via Supabase Storage. Create a document record with type='CR'. Step 2 (Profile Confirm): Insert into businesses table with user_id = auth.uid(). Create owner as first team_member. Step 3 (Contact): Update business record with phone/email/address. On completion: call generateObligations() from rules-engine.ts to auto-create compliance obligations. Redirect to /dashboard.
tasks.testStrategy: Complete full onboarding. Verify business record in DB. Verify CR document record created. Verify 5+ obligations auto-generated. Verify redirect to dashboard shows real data.

tasks.title: Wire Dashboard to Read Real Data
tasks.description: Replace all MOCK_ constants in dashboard with real Supabase queries. Show actual document counts, next obligation, and financial summary from the authenticated user's business.
tasks.details: Fetch business_id from businesses table where user_id = auth.uid(). Query documents for count by expiry status. Query obligations for next due and counts by status. Query transactions for money_in/money_out this month. Handle empty states for new users with no data yet. Keep Framer Motion animations.
tasks.testStrategy: After onboarding, dashboard shows real obligation counts. Upload a document — document count updates. Add a transaction — financial summary updates.

tasks.title: Wire Profile Page CRUD
tasks.description: Connect Business Profile page to read/update the businesses table. Edit mode saves changes. Track changes in profile_history JSONB.
tasks.details: Fetch business record on mount. Display all fields in 4 collapsible sections (Identity, Ownership, Contact, Branding). Edit mode: update businesses record. On save, append previous state to profile_history JSONB array with timestamp. Logo/stamp upload: upload to 'logos' storage bucket, save URL to logo_url/stamp_url columns.
tasks.testStrategy: Edit business name. Verify DB updated. Check profile_history has the old value. Upload logo — verify URL saved and image displays.

tasks.title: Wire Team Page CRUD
tasks.description: Connect Team page to team_members table. Add, edit, and deactivate team members with Saudi validation (Iqama, nationality). Show Saudization ratio.
tasks.details: Fetch team_members where business_id matches. Add member: insert with Iqama validation (10 digits, starts with 1 or 2). Edit member: update record. Terminate: set status='TERMINATED', termination_date=now(). Calculate and display Saudization ratio (Saudi count / total active). Toggle to show/hide terminated members.
tasks.testStrategy: Add team member with valid Iqama. Verify in DB. Edit salary. Terminate member. Verify Saudization ratio updates.

tasks.title: Wire Document Vault with Real File Upload
tasks.description: Connect vault page to documents table and Supabase Storage. Upload files, store metadata, display real documents with expiry tracking.
tasks.details: Fetch documents where business_id matches, is_current=true. Upload: send file to 'documents' storage bucket, create record with file_url, file_size, mime_type. Delete/archive: set archived_at. Search: filter by name/type. Sort by uploaded_at or expiry_date. Expiry badges: calculate from real expiry_date. After upload, trigger AI categorization (task 110).
tasks.testStrategy: Upload a PDF. Verify file in Supabase Storage. Verify document record with URL. Search by name. Archive document — disappears from list.

tasks.title: Wire Compliance Calendar with Real Obligations
tasks.description: Connect calendar page to obligations table. Display real obligations, allow manual creation, mark complete with recurring generation.
tasks.details: Fetch obligations where business_id matches. Display in list and month views. Add obligation: insert with type, name, frequency, next_due_date. Mark complete: set last_completed_at=now(). For recurring obligations (MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, ANNUAL), calculate and set next_due_date using getNextOccurrence(). Link obligations to documents via linked_document_id.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify auto-generated obligations show. Create manual obligation. Complete it — next_due_date advances for recurring. Filter by status works.

tasks.title: Wire Bookkeeper with Real Transactions
tasks.description: Connect bookkeeper page to transactions table. Display real financial data in summary cards and charts. Support manual transaction entry.
tasks.details: Fetch transactions where business_id matches. Calculate summary using calculations.ts functions with real data. Charts: pass real data to Recharts (category breakdown donut, monthly trend bar, cash flow area). Manual entry: insert transaction with type, amount, category, date, description. Period selector: filter by date range (this month, 3 months, 6 months, this year).
tasks.testStrategy: Add manual transaction. Summary cards update. Charts reflect real data. Period selector filters correctly.

tasks.title: Implement Real File Upload Component
tasks.description: Make FileUpload.tsx actually upload to Supabase Storage buckets. Support drag-drop, camera capture on mobile, progress indication, and file validation.
tasks.details: Use supabase.storage.from(bucket).upload() in FileUpload component. Generate unique file paths: {business_id}/{type}/{uuid}.{ext}. Validate: max 10MB, allowed types (PDF, PNG, JPG, CSV, XLSX). Show upload progress. On success, return the public URL. Camera capture: use navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia() on mobile for CR/receipt photos.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload a 5MB PDF — succeeds. Try 15MB — rejected. Upload via drag-drop. Upload via camera capture on mobile. Verify file exists in Supabase Storage.

tasks.title: Implement Claude API Document Analysis
tasks.description: Replace mock analyze-document endpoint with real Claude Vision API. Extract document type, expiry date, and key fields from uploaded images/PDFs.
tasks.details: In /api/analyze-document/route.ts: accept file URL or base64 image. Call Claude Vision API with a prompt to extract: document_type, expiry_date, issuing_authority, registration_number, and other relevant fields. Return structured JSON with ai_confidence score. Update document record with extracted_data JSONB and ai_confidence. If confidence < 0.7, flag for manual review. Handle Arabic text extraction.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload a real Saudi CR certificate image. Verify extracted type='CR', expiry_date parsed correctly, confidence > 0.7. Upload a GOSI certificate — verify different type detected.

tasks.title: Implement Claude API Bank Statement Parsing (CSV)
tasks.description: Build CSV bank statement parser using Claude Text API. Parse transactions from Saudi bank CSV formats, detect bank name, extract date/amount/description/type.
tasks.details: Create /api/parse-statement/route.ts. Accept CSV file content. Send to Claude API with prompt to: detect bank name, parse each row into {date, amount, type, description, vendor_or_client, category}. Handle multiple Saudi bank formats (Al Rajhi, SNB, Riyad Bank, SABB, etc.). Return array of parsed transactions with confidence scores. Insert into transactions table with source='CSV_IMPORT', source_file_id, is_reviewed=false for confidence < 0.7.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload Al Rajhi CSV. Verify bank detected. Verify transactions parsed with correct amounts and dates. Low-confidence items marked for review.

tasks.title: Implement Claude API Bank Statement Parsing (PDF)
tasks.description: Build PDF bank statement parser using Claude Vision API. Convert PDF pages to images and extract transactions.
tasks.details: Extend /api/parse-statement/route.ts to handle PDF. Use pdf-to-image conversion (pdf-lib or similar) to extract pages as images. Send each page image to Claude Vision API with extraction prompt. Merge results across pages, deduplicate. Same output format as CSV parser. Handle Arabic+English mixed content in statements.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload a multi-page PDF statement. Verify all pages processed. Verify transaction count matches expected. No duplicate entries across page boundaries.

tasks.title: Implement Claude API Receipt Extraction
tasks.description: Build receipt photo analyzer using Claude Vision API. Extract amount, vendor, date, category, and VAT from receipt photos.
tasks.details: Create /api/analyze-receipt/route.ts. Accept image (camera capture or upload). Call Claude Vision with prompt to extract: total_amount, vendor_name, date, category, vat_amount, line_items. Return structured JSON. Auto-create transaction record with source='RECEIPT' and receipt_url. If confidence < 0.7, show confirmation form before saving.
tasks.testStrategy: Photograph a Saudi receipt. Verify amount, vendor, date extracted. VAT amount detected. Transaction created in DB.

tasks.title: Implement Transaction Review Queue
tasks.description: Build the review queue for AI-parsed transactions with confidence < 0.7. Users must accept, edit, or reject before transactions are confirmed.
tasks.details: In bookkeeper upload flow: after parsing, show ReviewQueue component with unreviewed transactions. Display confidence bar, parsed values. Accept: set is_reviewed=true. Edit: update fields, set is_reviewed=true. Reject: delete transaction. Batch accept for high-confidence items. Filter bank statement upload status to show processing/completed/error states.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload statement with mixed confidence. Low-confidence items appear in review queue. Accept one — is_reviewed updates. Edit another — values change. Reject — deleted.

tasks.title: Auto-Link Documents to Obligations
tasks.description: When a document with an expiry date is uploaded, automatically create or update the corresponding compliance obligation.
tasks.details: After document analysis (task 110): if document has expiry_date and type maps to an obligation type (CR→CR_RENEWAL, GOSI_CERT→GOSI_MONTHLY, etc.), find or create the matching obligation. Set linked_document_id. Update obligation next_due_date if the document's expiry is more recent. When a document expires and is renewed, update the obligation chain.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload CR with expiry 2027-01-15. Verify CR_RENEWAL obligation created with due date near that expiry. Upload replacement — obligation updated.

tasks.title: Government Payment Detection
tasks.description: Automatically detect government payments in transactions (GOSI, ZATCA, municipality) and link them to corresponding obligations.
tasks.details: In transaction parsing: check description/vendor against government entity patterns (GOSI, ZATCA, municipality, MoL, etc.). When detected: set linked_obligation_id to the matching obligation. Optionally auto-complete the obligation if payment amount matches expected amount. Works for both CSV-imported and manually entered transactions.
tasks.testStrategy: Import CSV with GOSI payment. Verify transaction linked to GOSI obligation. Verify obligation marked as completed if amount matches.

tasks.title: Email Reminder System
tasks.description: Set up email service (Resend) and cron job to send compliance deadline reminders at 30/15/7/1 day intervals.
tasks.details: Install Resend SDK. Create email templates (bilingual AR/EN) for: obligation_reminder_30d, obligation_reminder_15d, obligation_reminder_7d, obligation_reminder_1d, document_expiry_warning. Create /api/cron/reminders route. Query obligations where next_due_date falls within reminder windows and corresponding reminder_sent flag is false. Send email, set flag. Use Vercel Cron (vercel.json) to run daily.
tasks.testStrategy: Create obligation due in 7 days. Run cron endpoint. Verify email sent. Verify reminder_7d_sent=true. Run again — no duplicate email.

tasks.title: VAT Estimation from Real Data
tasks.description: Calculate VAT liability estimation from actual transaction data. Show in bookkeeper dashboard.
tasks.details: Use calculateVATEstimate() from calculations.ts with real transaction data. Query transactions for the current VAT quarter. Calculate: total taxable income, total deductible VAT, estimated liability. Display in the existing VAT estimation card on bookkeeper page. Link to ZATCA obligation due date.
tasks.testStrategy: Add income and expense transactions with VAT. Verify VAT card shows correct calculation. Changes when period selector changes.

tasks.title: Hijri Date Display Across All Pages
tasks.description: Show Hijri dates alongside Gregorian dates in calendar, dashboard, and obligation listings. Use existing hijri.ts library.
tasks.details: In calendar page: show Hijri date next to each obligation's Gregorian date. In dashboard: show today's Hijri date in the welcome header. In calendar month view: show Hijri month/year. Format: '15 Shawwal 1447' alongside '16 Apr 2026'. Use toHijri() from lib/hijri.ts.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify dashboard shows Hijri date. Calendar obligations show dual dates. Month view header includes Hijri month.

tasks.title: Search and Filtering for Vault and Transactions
tasks.description: Implement real search by name and filtering by type/date/status in document vault and transaction list.
tasks.details: Vault: search input filters documents by name (client-side or ilike query). Filter by document type dropdown. Filter by status (valid/expiring/expired). Sort by date or name. Bookkeeper: filter transactions by category, type (income/expense), date range. Search by description or vendor. Pagination for large datasets.
tasks.testStrategy: With 10+ documents: search by partial name. Filter by type — shows subset. With 50+ transactions: filter by category, date range works.

tasks.title: Empty States for New Users
tasks.description: Design and implement empty state views for dashboard, vault, calendar, bookkeeper, and team when no data exists yet.
tasks.details: Dashboard: show onboarding prompt if no business exists. Show 'Upload your first document' if vault empty. Vault: illustration + 'No documents yet' + upload CTA. Calendar: 'Complete onboarding to see your obligations' or 'No obligations' + add CTA. Bookkeeper: 'Upload a bank statement or add your first transaction' + CTAs. Team: 'Add your first team member' + invite CTA. Use consistent design pattern across all pages.
tasks.testStrategy: Create fresh user with no data. Visit each page — see empty state with CTA. Click CTA — navigates to correct action.

tasks.title: Rate Limiting on AI Endpoints
tasks.description: Add rate limiting to AI-powered API routes to prevent abuse and control costs. 100 calls/user/day as per PRD.
tasks.details: Create rate-limit middleware for /api/analyze-document, /api/parse-statement, /api/analyze-receipt. Track calls per user_id per day in a simple counter (Supabase or in-memory). Return 429 Too Many Requests when limit exceeded. Show user-friendly message with reset time.
tasks.testStrategy: Call AI endpoint 100 times. 101st call returns 429. Wait until next day — resets.

tasks.title: Error Handling and Loading States
tasks.description: Add proper error boundaries, loading skeletons, and retry logic across all pages that fetch data.
tasks.details: Loading: skeleton loaders for dashboard cards, vault grid, calendar list, transaction table. Error: toast notifications for failed operations. Retry: exponential backoff for Supabase queries that fail. Network offline: show offline banner. Form validation errors: inline messages in Arabic and English.
tasks.testStrategy: Simulate slow network — skeletons show. Simulate API failure — error toast appears. Retry succeeds after transient failure.

tasks.title: Duplicate Detection
tasks.description: Prevent duplicate transactions (same date+amount+description) and duplicate CR numbers across businesses.
tasks.details: Transactions: before insert, query for existing transaction with same date, amount, description, business_id. If found, warn user and ask to confirm or skip. CR numbers: businesses.cr_number is already UNIQUE in schema, but add client-side validation with user-friendly error message on onboarding.
tasks.testStrategy: Import CSV with a duplicate transaction. Warning shown. User can skip or force-add. Try onboarding with existing CR number — shows error.

tasks.title: GOSI Monthly Recurrence Logic
tasks.description: When a GOSI monthly obligation is completed, automatically generate the next month's obligation.
tasks.details: In mark-complete handler: if obligation type is GOSI_MONTHLY and frequency is MONTHLY, calculate next month's due date using getNextOccurrence(). Create new obligation record with the next due date. Copy all fields except dates and completion status. This ensures GOSI obligations continue indefinitely, not just for the initial 12 months.
tasks.testStrategy: Complete April GOSI payment. Verify May obligation auto-created with correct due date. Complete May — June created.

tasks.title: Settings Page Persistence
tasks.description: Wire settings page toggles to persist preferences. Language switch, notification toggles, and theme preference should survive page reloads.
tasks.details: Store preferences in Supabase user metadata or a user_preferences table. Notification toggles: save to user record. Language: already handled by next-intl routing. Theme: store preference in localStorage + user record. Load preferences on page mount.
tasks.testStrategy: Toggle off weekly digest. Reload page — still off. Switch to Arabic — persists across sessions.

tasks.title: Mobile Responsive QA and Camera Support
tasks.description: Test and fix responsive layout on 375px+ mobile. Ensure camera capture works for CR upload and receipt photos on iOS/Android.
tasks.details: Test all pages at 375px, 390px, 414px widths. Fix any overflow, truncation, or touch target issues. Camera: use input[type=file][capture=environment] for mobile camera access. Test on real iOS Safari and Android Chrome. Verify drag-drop fallback on mobile (tap to select file).
tasks.testStrategy: Test on real phone or device emulator at 375px. Complete full flow: signup → onboarding (camera CR) → dashboard → upload receipt (camera). All interactions work with touch.

tasks.title: Security Audit and Hardening
tasks.description: Full security review: RLS enforcement, API key protection, input sanitization, CSRF, and rate limiting.
tasks.details: Verify: all Supabase queries go through RLS (no service_role key on client). ANTHROPIC_API_KEY only used server-side (never in client bundle). All user inputs sanitized before DB insert. File upload validates type and size. API routes check authentication. No SQL injection vectors. CORS configured correctly. CSP headers set.
tasks.testStrategy: Attempt to access another user's data via API — blocked by RLS. Check browser devtools — no API keys exposed. Upload malicious file — rejected. Send XSS payload in form — sanitized.

tasks.title: Final QA: Full User Journey Test
tasks.description: End-to-end test of the complete user journey in both Arabic and English. Every feature, every page, every edge case.
tasks.details: Test flow: 1) Sign up with email. 2) Complete onboarding with real CR upload. 3) Dashboard shows real obligations. 4) Upload documents to vault — AI categorizes. 5) Calendar shows auto-generated obligations. 6) Upload bank statement CSV — transactions parsed. 7) Review queue for low-confidence items. 8) Add manual transaction. 9) Bookkeeper charts show real data. 10) Add team member. 11) Switch to Arabic — everything works RTL. 12) Mobile test. 13) Sign out and back in — data persists.
tasks.testStrategy: Complete full journey as described above in both EN and AR. Document any issues found. All features work with real data.

tasks.title: Deploy Production with Environment Variables
tasks.description: Configure Vercel environment variables for Supabase and Anthropic API keys. Deploy to production. Verify all features work on the live URL.
tasks.details: Set on Vercel: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (for admin operations only), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Configure Vercel Cron for daily reminder job. Verify: auth flow works, file uploads work, AI analysis works, emails send. Test with real user account.
tasks.testStrategy: Full user journey on production URL. Sign up, onboard, upload docs, parse statements, check reminders. Everything works with real services.

- Create VersionHistoryPanel component
- Enhance AI analysis to detect renewals

tasks.title: Document Vault: Expiry Countdown Widgets & Auto-Renewal Reminders

tasks.title: Document Vault: Bulk Upload & OCR Text Search

tasks.title: Compliance Calendar: Penalty Calculator & Integration Readiness
tasks.description: Add estimated penalty amounts for late compliance and structure obligations for future integration with Absher, GOSI, and ZATCA portals
tasks.details:
1. Penalty calculation system:
   - Add penalty_rules table:

2. Penalty UI:
   - Show penalty estimate on overdue obligations
   - Add penalty calculator tool in calendar page
   - Display: "Estimated penalty: SAR X,XXX (Y days overdue)"
   - Add info tooltip explaining calculation

   - Add integration_config JSON column to businesses table

tasks.details:
**Implementation:**

1. **Bank Reconciliation:**

     - Unmatched bank statement transactions (from parse-statement)
     - Manual transactions without bank match
     - Suggested matches (by date ±3 days + amount ±1%)
   - Add `matched_statement_id` column to transactions table
   - Implement 3-way matching algorithm:
     - Exact match (date + amount)
     - Fuzzy match (date ±3d + amount ±1%)
     - Manual override

2. **Multi-Currency Support:**
   - Add `currency` (default 'SAR'), `amount_sar`, `exchange_rate` columns to transactions

     - Historical average cash flow by month

     - Factor in upcoming obligations (from calendar)
     - Linear regression for trend
   - Show 30-day forecast with confidence bands
   - Display forecast chart on bookkeeper dashboard
   - Alert if forecast shows negative balance in next 30 days

4. **Excel Import/Export:**
   - Support import from common Saudi bank formats:
     - Al Rajhi Bank CSV

tasks.title: Performance Optimization: Offline Mode, Caching & Sub-2s Load Times
tasks.description: Implement offline document viewing, aggressive caching strategy, code splitting, and ensure <2s page load times on 4G mobile
tasks.details:
**Implementation:**

1. **Offline Mode:**
   - Implement Service Worker using next-pwa ^5.6.0
   - Cache static assets (JS, CSS, images) with Cache API
   - Cache document metadata in IndexedDB
   - Pre-cache recently viewed documents (last 10)
   - Show offline indicator when no connection
   - Queue mutations (uploads, edits) when offline, sync on reconnect
   - Use Workbox strategies: NetworkFirst for API, CacheFirst for static

2. **Aggressive Caching:**
   - Implement multi-layer caching:
     - Browser cache (static assets, 1 year)
     - Service Worker cache (app shell, 1 week)
     - React Query cache (API responses, 5 minutes)
     - IndexedDB cache (documents, transactions, 24 hours)
   - Use stale-while-revalidate for non-critical data
   - Implement cache invalidation on mutations
   - Add Cache-Control headers in Next.js config

3. **Code Splitting:**
   - Use Next.js dynamic imports for heavy components:
     - Chart libraries (recharts) → lazy load
     - PDF viewer → lazy load
     - AI components → lazy load
   - Split by route (already done by Next.js App Router)
   - Use React.lazy + Suspense for large components
   - Analyze bundle with next-bundle-analyzer
   - Target <200KB initial JS bundle

4. **Performance Optimizations:**
   - Image optimization:
     - Use Next.js Image component (already configured)
     - Convert images to WebP/AVIF (already in config)
     - Lazy load images below fold
   - Database query optimization:
     - Add indexes for frequently queried columns
     - Use Supabase select() with specific columns
     - Implement pagination (limit 50 per page)
   - API optimization:
     - Batch Claude API requests where possible
     - Implement request debouncing for search
   - Reduce Tailwind bundle:
     - Use @apply sparingly
     - Configure PurgeCSS (already done in Tailwind v4)

5. **Monitoring:**
   - Add Vercel Analytics (free tier)
   - Track Core Web Vitals: LCP, FID, CLS
   - Set performance budgets:
     - LCP < 2.5s
     - FID < 100ms
     - CLS < 0.1
   - Add error tracking with Sentry (optional)

**Libraries:**
- next-pwa ^5.6.0 (new) for PWA/offline support
- @tanstack/react-query ^5.0.0 (new) for data caching
- idb ^8.0.0 (new) for IndexedDB wrapper
- @vercel/analytics ^1.0.0 (new) for monitoring

**Next.js Config:**
```typescript
// next.config.ts
import withPWA from 'next-pwa'

const config = {
  // ... existing config
  reactStrictMode: true,
  compress: true,
  swcMinify: true,
  experimental: {
    optimizeCss: true,
    optimizePackageImports: ['lucide-react', 'recharts', '@radix-ui/react-*']
  }
}

export default withPWA({
  dest: 'public',
  register: true,
  skipWaiting: true,
  disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'
})(config)
```

tasks.title: Build Document Relationship Mapping System
tasks.description: Detect when uploaded documents relate to existing ones (renewed licenses, related certificates) and auto-suggest document linking
tasks.details:
When user uploads a renewed CR (same CR number, newer expiry), system should detect the old CR and suggest linking/replacing. When GOSI cert uploaded, detect if it references the business CR number.

Implementation:
1. Create src/lib/documents/relationship-detector.ts module
2. After document analysis completes, run relationship detection:
   - Query documents table for same document_type and business_id
   - Compare registration_number fields (if old CR has number 1234567890 and new CR has same, they're related)
   - Compare dates: if expiry_date of new doc > old doc, it's a renewal
   - Check if additional_data contains references to other documents (e.g., GOSI cert mentions CR number)
3. Store relationships in new document_relationships table (schema: id, source_doc_id, target_doc_id, relationship_type ['RENEWAL', 'REFERENCE', 'SUPERSEDES'], confidence, created_at)
4. Create API endpoint /api/documents/suggest-links POST that returns suggested links for a document
5. In vault UI (src/app/[locale]/(app)/vault/page.tsx), after upload show banner: "This document appears to be a renewal of [old CR link]. Archive old version?"
6. Implement auto-archive: when user confirms, set is_current=false on old doc, archived_at=now()

Database migration:
CREATE TABLE document_relationships (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  source_document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  target_document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  relationship_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (relationship_type IN ('RENEWAL', 'REFERENCE', 'SUPERSEDES', 'RELATED')),
  detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  confidence NUMERIC(3,2) CHECK (confidence >= 0 AND confidence <= 1)
);

Algorithm:
- RENEWAL: same type + same reg number + newer date
- REFERENCE: doc A's additional_data contains doc B's registration number
- SUPERSEDES: explicit "replaces" or "cancels" language in extracted text
- RELATED: same issuing_authority + overlapping date ranges
tasks.testStrategy: Upload CR with reg_number 1234567890, expiry 2025-12-31. Upload new CR with same reg_number, expiry 2026-12-31. Verify system detects RENEWAL relationship with confidence > 0.8. Upload GOSI cert that mentions CR 1234567890 in text. Verify REFERENCE relationship detected. Confirm UI shows suggestion banner. Test auto-archive flow - old doc should have is_current=false after confirmation.

- INSURANCE → create INSURANCE obligation (annual, due on policy expiry)

Prompt enhancement:
"After extracting document data, analyze compliance implications for a Saudi business:
- Does this document create a legal obligation? (lease, permit, license)
- Does it require periodic renewal or payment?
- Are there deadlines or due dates mentioned?
Return compliance_implications array with: {type, description, suggested_due_date, confidence}"

tasks.title: Enhance Receipt/Invoice Parsing for Handwritten Text and Complex Tax Rates
tasks.description: Improve AI receipt parsing to handle handwritten receipts, multiple VAT rates (15%/zero-rated), and detect VAT registration numbers

VAT number validation:

tasks.title: Implement PDF Bank Statement Parsing with Table Extraction
tasks.description: Add support for parsing bank statements in PDF format (not just CSV), with intelligent table extraction and Saudi bank layout recognition
tasks.details:
Current state: parse-statement route.ts only handles CSV input. Saudi banks (Al Rajhi, SNB, Riyad Bank) provide PDF statements. Need PDF table extraction.

Implementation:
1. Create new API route src/app/api/parse-statement-pdf/route.ts
2. Accept PDF file as base64 or URL (similar to analyze-document)
3. Use Claude's document processing with enhanced prompt for table extraction:
   "This is a Saudi bank statement PDF. Extract the transaction table. For each row, identify: date, description, debit/credit indicator, amount, balance. Bank layouts vary:
   - Al Rajhi: Arabic-heavy, columns right-to-left
   - SNB/Riyad: English columns, left-to-right
   - SABB: Mixed Arabic/English
   Detect the bank from header/logo. Parse all transaction rows, skipping headers and summary rows."
4. Response schema matches existing ParseStatementResponse from CSV route
5. Add bank_detected field to identify which Saudi bank (from SAUDI_BANKS list)
6. Handle multi-page statements (statements often 3-10 pages)
7. In bookkeeper upload UI, accept both .csv and .pdf files
8. Route to appropriate parser based on file type

Claude PDF table extraction approach:
- Document block type processes PDFs natively
- Claude can recognize table structures visually
- Prompt must be explicit: "Read the table row by row. Each row is one transaction."
- Handle Arabic column headers: تاريخ (date), مبلغ (amount), وصف (description)

Edge cases:
- Multi-currency statements (SAR, USD) - extract currency and convert
- Statements with summary sections - skip these, only extract transactions
- Scanned PDFs (image-based) vs native PDFs - Claude handles both

Performance:
- PDF parsing uses more tokens than CSV (4096+ tokens likely)
- May hit rate limit faster - consider increasing DAILY_LIMIT for PDF uploads
- Cache parsing results in bank_statement_uploads table

tasks.title: Build Smart Transaction Categorization with Learning from User Corrections
tasks.description: Implement ML-like categorization that learns from user corrections - when user re-categorizes a transaction, remember pattern for similar future transactions
tasks.details:
Current state: Claude categorizes transactions but doesn't learn from corrections. Need: feedback loop where user corrections improve future categorization.

Implementation:
1. Create categorization_corrections table:
   CREATE TABLE categorization_corrections (
     id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     original_description TEXT NOT NULL,
     original_category TEXT NOT NULL,
     corrected_category TEXT NOT NULL,
     vendor_pattern TEXT,
     correction_count INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
     created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
     updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
   );
   CREATE INDEX idx_cat_corrections_business_vendor ON categorization_corrections(business_id, vendor_pattern);

2. When user changes transaction category in UI (ReviewQueue.tsx):
   - Call POST /api/transactions/learn-category
   - Extract vendor pattern from description (e.g., "SABB ATM WITHDRAWAL" → vendor_pattern: "SABB ATM")
   - Upsert to categorization_corrections (increment correction_count if exists)

3. Enhance categorization logic in parse-statement and analyze-receipt routes:
   - Before calling Claude, query categorization_corrections for matching patterns
   - If vendor_pattern matches description (fuzzy match) and correction_count ≥ 2, use corrected_category
   - Pass corrections as context to Claude: "User has previously categorized similar transactions: [examples]. Prefer these categories when applicable."

4. Create src/lib/bookkeeper/smart-categorizer.ts:
   - function findMatchingCorrection(businessId, description): Promise<Correction | null>
   - Use fuzzy string matching (Levenshtein distance <3 or includes matching)
   - Return correction if confidence > 0.8

5. In ReviewQueue UI, show "Learning" badge when system applies a learned correction

Vendor pattern extraction:
- "SABB BANK FEE 123" → "SABB BANK FEE"
- "JARIR BOOKSTORE RIYADH" → "JARIR BOOKSTORE"
- "Transfer to Ahmed" → "Transfer" (too generic, skip)
- Algorithm: extract first 2-3 significant words (length > 3), remove numbers/dates

Libraries:
- Consider: string-similarity npm package for fuzzy matching
- Or: simple includes() check if exact substring match

Fallback: if no correction found, rely on Claude's original categorization

Verify auto-categorized as BANK_FEES (learned pattern). Correct 3 different JARIR transactions to SUPPLIES. Upload new JARIR transaction. Confirm categorized as SUPPLIES without Claude call (direct pattern match). Check categorization_corrections table shows correction_count=3 for JARIR. Test fuzzy match: "JARIR BOOKSTORE" vs "JARIR BOOK STORE" should match. Verify Learning badge appears in UI when correction applied.

tasks.title: Implement Duplicate Transaction Detection Across Sources
tasks.description: Detect when same transaction appears multiple times (e.g., manually entered then appears in bank statement) and flag for user review
tasks.details:
Common scenario: user manually enters rent payment 5000 SAR on 2025-01-05. Later uploads bank statement with same transaction. Need to detect and offer merge/delete.

Implementation:
1. Enhance existing src/lib/bookkeeper/duplicate-detection.ts (if exists) or create new module
2. After transaction insertion, run duplicate detection:
   - Query transactions table for same business_id
   - Match criteria:
     * Same date (±2 days tolerance)
     * Same amount (exact match)
     * Similar description (fuzzy match >80%)
     * Same type (INCOME/EXPENSE)
   - If match found and sources differ (e.g., MANUAL + BANK_STATEMENT_CSV), flag as potential duplicate

3. Create duplicates table:
   CREATE TABLE transaction_duplicates (
     id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     transaction_id_1 UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES transactions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     transaction_id_2 UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES transactions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     similarity_score NUMERIC(3,2) NOT NULL,
     resolved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
     resolution_action TEXT,
     created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
   );

4. Create API endpoint POST /api/transactions/resolve-duplicate:
   - Actions: 'keep_first', 'keep_second', 'merge', 'keep_both'
   - If merge: combine descriptions, keep higher confidence transaction
   - If keep_one: soft-delete the other (set is_reviewed=true, add note "Duplicate removed")

5. In ReviewQueue UI (src/components/bookkeeper/ReviewQueue.tsx):
   - Query unresolved duplicates
   - Show warning banner: "Possible duplicate: [tx1] and [tx2]. Review?"
   - Provide action buttons: Keep First, Keep Second, Merge, Keep Both

6. Duplicate detection algorithm:
   - Date match: |date1 - date2| ≤ 2 days → score +0.4
   - Amount match: amount1 === amount2 → score +0.4
   - Description similarity: leven(desc1, desc2) / max(len(desc1), len(desc2)) → score +0.2
   - Threshold: score ≥ 0.8 = likely duplicate

Edge cases:
- Recurring transactions (12 monthly rent payments) - don't flag as duplicates if dates >30 days apart
- Round numbers (5000, 10000) - higher false positive risk, require description match
- Same vendor multiple times per day - check description + amount precisely

Libraries: Use built-in string comparison or date-fns for date tolerance
tasks.testStrategy: Manually create transaction: date=2025-01-05, amount=5000, type=EXPENSE, description="Rent payment", source=MANUAL. Upload bank statement with: date=2025-01-05, amount=5000, description="Rent to landlord". Verify duplicate detected with similarity_score ≥ 0.8. Test UI shows duplicate warning. Select "Keep Second" action. Confirm first transaction marked as resolved, second remains active. Create two legitimate transactions: same vendor, same amount, 45 days apart. Verify NOT flagged as duplicate (date tolerance). Test edge case: two different vendors, same amount 1000, same day. Should NOT flag (description mismatch).

tasks.title: Build Financial Forecasting Engine with Cash Burn Prediction
tasks.description: Analyze historical transaction data to predict monthly cash burn, project cash reserves depletion, identify seasonal patterns, and suggest cost optimizations
tasks.details:
Use AI to analyze transaction history and provide actionable financial insights. This is a new intelligence module, not currently implemented.

Implementation:

2. Create API endpoint POST /api/analytics/forecast requiring businessId
3. Query transactions table for last 12 months of data
4. Prepare financial summary:
   - Monthly revenue (sum of INCOME transactions per month)
   - Monthly expenses by category (sum of EXPENSE transactions grouped by category)
   - Net cash flow per month (revenue - expenses)
   - Current cash balance (from latest BANK_STATEMENT or manual input)

5. Send summary to Claude with forecasting prompt:
   "You are a financial analyst for a Saudi SME. Analyze the following 12-month transaction history:
   [data]
   Provide:
   - Monthly cash burn rate trend (increasing/decreasing/stable)
   - Forecast next 3 months expenses based on trend
   - If current cash balance is X SAR and burn rate is Y SAR/month, when will reserves run low?
   - Identify seasonal patterns (e.g., Q4 revenue spike, summer expense dip)
   - Cost optimization opportunities (categories with unusual spending)
   Return JSON: {burn_rate_monthly, forecast_3m, cash_depletion_date, seasonal_patterns[], optimization_suggestions[]}"

6. Response schema:
   interface ForecastResult {
     burn_rate_monthly: number
     forecast_next_3_months: { month: string, projected_expense: number }[]
     cash_depletion_date: string | null  // ISO date when balance → 0
     cash_depletion_days: number | null  // days until depletion
     seasonal_patterns: { pattern: string, confidence: number }[]
     optimization_suggestions: { category: string, current_monthly_avg: number, suggested_target: number, reasoning: string }[]
     confidence: number
   }

7. Display forecast in dashboard (src/app/[locale]/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx):
   - "Cash Runway: 6.2 months" card
   - "Projected Q2 Burn: 45,000 SAR" card
   - Optimization suggestions as dismissible alerts

8. Alert logic:
   - If cash_depletion_days < 90, show WARNING alert
   - If cash_depletion_days < 30, show CRITICAL alert + email notification
   - If optimization_suggestions.length > 0, show actionable tips

Data requirements:
- Minimum 3 months of transaction history for basic forecast
- 12+ months for seasonal pattern detection
- If insufficient data, return graceful message: "Need 3+ months of data"

Claude model choice: Use claude-sonnet-4 (current) for cost-effectiveness. Financial analysis doesn't require Opus.

Caching: Cache forecast results for 24 hours (store in cache table or Redis). Recalculate daily or when significant new transactions added (>10% of monthly volume).

Verify burn_rate_monthly ≈ 16.5k, cash_depletion_date ≈ 3-4 months out. Check seasonal_patterns detects any repeated monthly spikes. Add scenario: business with 2 months data. Verify graceful response (insufficient data). Test optimization: seed heavy MARKETING spend (10k/month). Confirm suggestions include reducing marketing. Validate forecast accuracy: compare forecast_next_3_months[0] against actual transactions from next month (if available in test data). Test dashboard UI displays Cash Runway card and critical alert when depletion < 30 days.

tasks.title: Implement Rate Limiting with Response Caching and Confidence-Based Review Flagging
tasks.description: Enhance rate limiting system, add response caching for identical inputs, implement confidence scoring threshold for user review flags, and add graceful degradation
tasks.details:
Current state: rate-limit.ts implements 100 calls/day limit. Need: response caching, tiered limits, low-confidence flagging, fallback handling.

Implementation:
1. Response Caching:
   - Create ai_response_cache table:
     CREATE TABLE ai_response_cache (
       id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
       input_hash TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
       endpoint TEXT NOT NULL,
       response_data JSONB NOT NULL,
       confidence NUMERIC(3,2),
       created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
       hit_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0
     );
     CREATE INDEX idx_cache_hash ON ai_response_cache(input_hash);
   - Before calling Claude API, compute SHA-256 hash of: endpoint + fileUrl/base64Data + prompt
   - Query cache: if found and created_at < 7 days ago, return cached response (increment hit_count)
   - After Claude response, store in cache with TTL 7 days
   - Implement cache cleanup job: DELETE FROM ai_response_cache WHERE created_at < now() - interval '7 days'

2. Tiered Rate Limits:
   - Current: 100/day for all businesses
   - Add business_tier column to businesses table: 'FREE' (100/day), 'BASIC' (300/day), 'PRO' (1000/day)
   - Modify checkRateLimit to read tier from businesses table
   - Return rate limit tier in response headers: X-RateLimit-Tier, X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining

3. Confidence-Based Review Flagging:
   - After document/receipt analysis, check ai_confidence
   - If ai_confidence < 0.7, set needs_review=true flag
   - Add needs_review column to documents and transactions tables
   - In vault/bookkeeper UI, show "Low Confidence" badge and move to review queue
   - Track review completion: user reviews, corrects data, sets needs_review=false

4. Graceful Degradation:
   - If Claude API returns 429 (rate limit) or 503 (service unavailable):
     * Log error
     * Return fallback response with ai_confidence=0
     * Set needs_review=true
     * Show user message: "AI unavailable, please review and correct manually"
   - If API timeout (>30 seconds):
     * Retry once with exponential backoff (wait 5 seconds)
     * If still fails, return fallback

5. Monitoring:
   - Log all AI calls to ai_usage_log table:
     CREATE TABLE ai_usage_log (
       id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
       business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id),
       endpoint TEXT NOT NULL,
       cached BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
       duration_ms INTEGER,
       tokens_used INTEGER,
       cost_usd NUMERIC(8,4),
       created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
     );
   - Track: daily usage per business, cache hit rate, average confidence, API latency
   - Admin dashboard: view usage trends, identify businesses hitting limits

Libraries:
- crypto.createHash('sha256') for input hashing (Node.js built-in)
- Anthropic SDK usage tracking: response.usage.input_tokens, response.usage.output_tokens

Cost calculation (approximate):
- Claude Sonnet 4: $3/MTok input, $15/MTok output
- Typical document analysis: 2000 input tokens (image) + 800 output = $0.018
- Cache saves ~100% of cost for duplicate uploads

Confidence thresholds:
- ≥ 0.9: high confidence, auto-accept
- 0.7-0.89: medium confidence, accept but available for review
- < 0.7: low confidence, flag for mandatory review
tasks.testStrategy: Upload same document twice. First call hits Claude API, second retrieves from cache. Verify cache hit_count increments. Check response time: cache retrieval <100ms vs API call >2000ms. Simulate rate limit by setting DAILY_LIMIT=1, make 2 calls. Verify second returns 429 error gracefully. Upload blurry receipt. Expect ai_confidence < 0.7, needs_review=true. Check UI shows Low Confidence badge. Query ai_usage_log after 10 API calls. Verify tokens_used and cost_usd tracked accurately. Test fallback: mock Claude API to return 503. Verify system returns fallback response with confidence=0. Test tier limits: set business to PRO tier, verify X-RateLimit-Limit header shows 1000.

tasks.title: Create chat database schema and Supabase migrations
tasks.description: Design and implement database schema for AI chat conversations, messages, file attachments, and conversation context
tasks.details:
Create Supabase tables:

1. `conversations` table:
   - id (uuid, pk)
   - business_id (uuid, fk to businesses)
   - title (text, nullable - auto-generated from first message)
   - created_at (timestamp)
   - updated_at (timestamp)
   - last_message_at (timestamp)

2. `chat_messages` table:
   - id (uuid, pk)
   - conversation_id (uuid, fk)
   - role (enum: 'user', 'assistant')
   - content (text)
   - message_type (enum: 'text', 'action_confirmation', 'action_result', 'excel_summary', 'data_query_result')
   - metadata (jsonb - for storing rich content: tables, charts, action buttons, file references)
   - created_at (timestamp)
   - Index on (conversation_id, created_at DESC)

3. `chat_file_uploads` table:
   - id (uuid, pk)
   - conversation_id (uuid, fk)
   - message_id (uuid, fk to chat_messages, nullable)
   - file_url (text - Supabase Storage path)
   - file_name (text)
   - file_type (text - mime type)
   - file_size (int)
   - upload_status (enum: 'pending', 'processed', 'failed')
   - processing_result (jsonb - stores parsed Excel data or AI extraction results)
   - created_at (timestamp)

4. `chat_actions` table:
   - id (uuid, pk)
   - message_id (uuid, fk)
   - action_type (enum: 'import_transactions', 'add_team_member', 'upload_document', 'create_obligation', 'generate_report')
   - action_payload (jsonb)
   - status (enum: 'pending', 'confirmed', 'executed', 'cancelled')
   - result (jsonb - stores action outcome)
   - created_at (timestamp)
   - executed_at (timestamp, nullable)

Add RLS policies for all tables to ensure users can only access their own business's conversations.

Create indexes for performance:
- conversations(business_id, last_message_at DESC)
- chat_messages(conversation_id, created_at DESC)
- chat_file_uploads(conversation_id)

Use Supabase CLI to generate migration: `supabase migration new chat_schema`
tasks.testStrategy:
Write integration tests:
1. Create conversation and verify business_id isolation
2. Insert messages in sequence and query chronologically
3. Upload file reference and link to message
4. Test RLS policies by attempting cross-business access (should fail)
5. Verify indexes improve query performance on large datasets
6. Test cascade deletes when conversation is removed

tasks.title: Add comprehensive security, rate limiting, and monitoring
tasks.description: Implement security measures for AI actions, enhance rate limiting, add audit logging, and set up monitoring for AI costs and performance
tasks.details:
**Security Enhancements:**

1. **Action Confirmation Requirements** (`src/lib/chat/security.ts`):
```typescript
export enum ActionRiskLevel {
  LOW = 'low', // Read-only queries
  MEDIUM = 'medium', // Create/update single record
  HIGH = 'high', // Bulk operations, delete, financial
  CRITICAL = 'critical', // Delete business data, bulk delete
}

export function getActionRiskLevel(actionName: string): ActionRiskLevel {
  const riskLevels: Record<string, ActionRiskLevel> = {
    add_transaction: ActionRiskLevel.MEDIUM,
    add_team_member: ActionRiskLevel.MEDIUM,
    import_excel_data: ActionRiskLevel.HIGH,
    delete_transaction: ActionRiskLevel.HIGH,
    update_team_member: ActionRiskLevel.MEDIUM,
    bulk_delete: ActionRiskLevel.CRITICAL,
  };
  return riskLevels[actionName] || ActionRiskLevel.LOW;
}

export function requiresExplicitConfirmation(actionName: string): boolean {
  const level = getActionRiskLevel(actionName);
  return level === ActionRiskLevel.HIGH || level === ActionRiskLevel.CRITICAL;
}
```

2. **Enhanced Rate Limiting:**
Extend `src/lib/rate-limit.ts`:
- Tier-based limits: Free (10 AI calls/day), Pro (100/day), Enterprise (unlimited)
- Separate limits for different operations (chat: 50/day, Excel parsing: 10/day)
- Cost tracking per business
- Block after suspicious activity

3. **Audit Logging** (new table: `ai_audit_logs`):
```sql
CREATE TABLE ai_audit_logs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  conversation_id UUID REFERENCES conversations(id),
  action_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  action_payload JSONB,
  risk_level TEXT NOT NULL,
  confirmation_required BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
  user_confirmed BOOLEAN,
  executed BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
  result JSONB,
  error TEXT,
  claude_model TEXT,
  tokens_used INT,
  cost_estimate DECIMAL(10, 4),
  ip_address INET,
  user_agent TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_logs_business ON ai_audit_logs(business_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_logs_action ON ai_audit_logs(action_type, created_at DESC);
```

Log every AI interaction and action execution.

4. **Input Sanitization:**
- Validate all user inputs before sending to Claude
- Prevent prompt injection attacks
- Sanitize file uploads
- Limit message length (max 10,000 chars)

5. **Output Validation:**
- Never expose raw database queries to AI
- Validate AI-generated action payloads against schemas
- Sanitize AI responses before rendering (XSS prevention)

6. **Monitoring Dashboard:**
Create `/api/admin/ai-metrics` endpoint:
- Total AI calls per day
- Average response time
- Error rate
- Cost per business
- Most used features
- Token usage trends

Integrate with Sentry for error tracking:
```typescript
import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs';

try {
  const response = await anthropic.messages.create(...);
} catch (error) {
  Sentry.captureException(error, {
    tags: { feature: 'ai-chat' },
    extra: { businessId, conversationId },
  });
  throw error;
}
```

7. **Cost Management:**
- Track Claude API costs per business
- Alert admins when costs exceed thresholds
- Implement token usage optimization (shorter system prompts when possible)
- Cache common queries

**Environment Variables:**
```env
CLAUDE_API_KEY=...
CLAUDE_MAX_TOKENS_PER_REQUEST=4096
RATE_LIMIT_FREE_TIER=10
RATE_LIMIT_PRO_TIER=100
AI_COST_ALERT_THRESHOLD=100
SENTRY_DSN=...
```
